brief_excerpt: Up to date account and analysis of the Iran-USA-Israel war in 2026 and its implications on the US economy. Cover: current military situation and key events, oil market impact (prices, supply disruption, Strait of Hormuz), broader US economic effects (inflation, energy costs, market reaction), and geopolitical context.

key_sources.title: Analysts hike oil price outlooks as Iran conflict enters third week
key_sources.summary: Iran's new Supreme Leader vowed to keep Hormuz shut; Brent and WTI headed for 10%+ weekly gains in week 3; analysts raised price targets toward $100+ and $120+ scenarios.

key_sources.title: How Iran Blocking the Strait of Hormuz Affects the U.S.
key_sources.summary: Rates Trump's claim that Strait closure 'doesn't really affect' the US as misleading; documents 50-cent+ gasoline spike and IEA data on Strait traffic slowing to a trickle.

key_sources.title: Iran Conflict and the Strait of Hormuz: Impacts on Oil, Gas, and Other Commodities
key_sources.summary: CRS report documenting US-Israeli military operations against Iran since February 2026, oil price history including Jun 2025 +8.5% spike, and Strait of Hormuz closure mechanics as of March 2026.

key_sources.title: Oil prices sink 13% as Trump predicts Middle East de-escalation
key_sources.summary: Brent fell 12.6% to $86.50/bbl on March 10 after Trump said war was 'very complete'; both benchmarks had hit $119/bbl on March 9.

key_sources.title: Oil prices surge 13% in first trades after start of US-Iran conflict
key_sources.summary: Brent hit $82.37 intraday on Day 1 (+13% from prior close of ~$72.80); confirmed 30% YTD gain from Jan 1 2026 baseline of ~$61/bbl.

key_sources.title: Two pipelines helping Saudi Arabia, UAE bypass the Strait of Hormuz
key_sources.summary: Saudi Petroline capacity up to 7 mbd ramping toward full; UAE ADCOP at 71% utilization with ~440k bpd spare; Ruwais refinery fire limits UAE upside.

key_sources.title: How attack on Iran could impact global oil market and economy
key_sources.summary: Kpler data: >14M bbl/day through Strait in 2025 (⅓ of seaborne exports); expert Robert McNally: 'A prolonged closure is a guaranteed global recession'; Brent at $72.48 day before conflict.

key_sources.title: How the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran will test the stock market, economy
key_sources.summary: Documents crude oil +35% in first week post-Strait closure; S&P 500 –2%, Europe –7%, EM –8.4% in first 5 trading days; energy sector is only 3.4% of S&P 500.

key_sources.title: Iran war threatens prolonged impact on energy markets as oil prices rise
key_sources.summary: Reports ~20% of global crude/LNG supply suspended, ~140M barrels frozen in Gulf ports, oil prices surged >25% since war start, and political vulnerability for Trump ahead of midterms.

key_sources.title: What does the US-Israel attack on Iran mean for oil prices?
key_sources.summary: Reports first Iranian Strait closure since US military threats; second live-fire drill in Strait in recent weeks; Spain condemns US-Israeli strikes.

open_questions.question: Was the Ruwais refinery fire caused by Iranian strikes or an internal accident? If Iranian-caused, does ADNOC have realistic ability to restore UAE bypass capacity?
open_questions.note: CNBC reported Ruwais closed due to fire; this materially caps UAE ADCOP bypass at <1.8 mbd and changes the supply gap arithmetic.

open_questions.question: What is the estimated duration of Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG shutdown, and have European TTF gas prices already reflected this disruption?
open_questions.note: Qatar LNG halt has direct European energy security implications distinct from the crude oil story.

open_questions.question: What is Iran's current military posture and capability to sustain Strait disruption — mines, missiles, naval assets?
open_questions.note: Critical for duration-of-shock estimate which drives recession vs. soft-landing scenarios.

open_questions.question: What is the US SPR (Strategic Petroleum Reserve) current level and release capacity, and has the administration announced a release?
open_questions.note: A major policy lever that could cap oil prices; Trump mentioned 'risk insurance' for tankers but SPR deployment not confirmed in sources.

open_questions.question: Is Saudi Arabia strategically limiting OPEC+ production increases to maximize revenue windfall from the Hormuz closure, given its Red Sea export route is unaffected?
open_questions.note: The 206k bpd increase vs. 4.1 mbd spare is conspicuously small. Saudi incentive structure under Hormuz closure is a key geopolitical question.
